text: Box Office: ‘Reminders of Him’ Leads on Friday With $8 Million; ‘Hoppers’ Holds Strong With $7.1 Million - Variety
fullText: Disney and Pixar's “Hoppers” is holding strong in its second weekend, but it was Universal's "Reminders of Him" that took the top spot on Friday.

text: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company - TechCrunch

text: Pope Leo takes possession of apartment in Apostolic Palace - Vatican News
fullText: The Director of the Holy See Press Office confirms Pope Leo XIV’s move to the Apostolic Palace after having resided at the Palazzo del Sant’Uffizio since the beginning of his pontificate.

text: Sinema admits a romance with her security guard as she fights ex-wife’s lawsuit - The Seattle Times
fullText: Kyrsten Sinema says in a legal document that she had a romantic relationship with a member of her security detail, but her lawyer argues a lawsuit seeking damages against her should be dismissed.

text: Trump claims international coalition will send war ships to reopen the strait of Hormuz - Axios
fullText: U.S., Israeli and Western officials say the situation at the strait has been the key concern for the Trump administration in recent days.

text: Exclusive: Ric Grenell departing as Kennedy Center head - Axios

text: Trump says U.S. struck Kharg Island, core of Iran’s oil economy - The Washington Post

text: Senate Republicans worry they’re losing ground in the midterms - Politico

text: Toto Wolff's 'Lewis Hamilton's still our driver' comment melts F1 fans after Mercedes 1-2 in China - Motorsport.com

text: As the risk of measles grows, why are parents so divided on vaccines? - NPR
fullText: In South Carolina, some parents embrace vaccines, others opt out. Why do people make such different choices? A mix of politics, distrust and misinformation is pushing neighbors apart.

text: Ray Dalio: I've studied 500 years of history and fear we're entering the most dangerous phase of the 'Big Cycle' - Fortune
fullText: I believe the times ahead will be radically different from what most people have gotten used to—more like the era before 1945 than since.

text: House GOP leadership silent as more members post anti-Muslim statements - NPR
fullText: A growing number of Republicans in Congress are embracing rhetoric against Muslims. Their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.

text: TSA workers grapple with loss of first paycheck: ‘I don’t want to depend on anybody else’ - CNN
fullText: A father and Transportation Security Administration officer said he “crumbled” when one of his children asked whether he needed money in the wake of the nearly four-week-long government shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

text: Refocused US rebounds from Italy shocker, beats Canada to reach WBC semifinals - AP News
fullText: The United States is heading to the semifinals of the World Baseball Classic after a “different level of focus” helped this All-Star-studded team rebound from a shocking loss. The Americans beat Canada 5-3 on Friday night for a spot in the semifinals Sunday a…

text: Apple hosts 50th anniversary celebrations around the world - Apple
fullText: Apple kicked off celebrations in New York for its 50th anniversary and will continue commemorating the milestone throughout the month.
